cards.front: What is the Candidate Elimination algorithm?
cards.back: A method for approximate inference in Bayesian networks, used to eliminate variables from consideration based on the distribution of their parents

cards.front: What is the goal of the Candidate Elimination algorithm?
cards.back: To reduce the size of the search space by eliminating variables that cannot contribute to the calculation of the target distribution

cards.front: What type of variables are eliminated first in the Candidate Elimination algorithm?
cards.back: Variables with a single parent, as they can be eliminated immediately

cards.front: What is the result of the Candidate Elimination algorithm?
cards.back: A reduced set of possible values for the target variable, making it easier to calculate the target distribution

cards.front: Who first proposed the Candidate Elimination algorithm?
